note: Patient is a 54-year-old male presenting for follow-up of hypertension. Blood pressure today 138/86. Continues lisinopril 10mg daily. Plan: continue current regimen, recheck in 3 months.
transcript: Doctor: Your pressure today is 138 over 86. Any chest pain? Patient: No. Doctor: Let's keep you on the 10 milligrams and recheck in three months.

note: Patient reports a productive cough for one week with yellow sputum. Temperature 100.8F. Assessment: acute bronchitis. Prescribed amoxicillin 500mg three times daily for 7 days.
transcript: Doctor: How long has the cough been going on? Patient: About a week, coughing up yellow stuff. Doctor: You're at 100.8 today. Sounds like bronchitis — amoxicillin 500 three times a day for a week.

note: Patient here for medication refill. Reports good adherence to metformin 1000mg twice daily. Last A1c was 6.9. Plan: refill metformin, repeat A1c at next visit.
transcript: Doctor: Are you taking the metformin twice a day? Patient: Yes, 1000 milligrams morning and night. Doctor: Your last A1c was 6.9. I'll refill it and recheck next time.
